title: rubus × dealbatus
intro: Rubus × dealbatus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or white-thorn raspberry, is a hybrid of the Rubus idaeus (European raspberry) and Rubus aculeatissimus (Himalayan raspberry), characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and edible, white or pale yellow berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × dealbatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The plant's berries, leaves, and roots can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and promote wound healing.
